source_of_truth: docs/feature/client-driven-onboarding/distill/roadmap.json (CDO-S5 entry) + ADR-048 + ADR-050 §a-§f + design/application-architecture.md (file-by-file map + §c retry table + §e.5 session-chat vocabulary)
paradigm: object-oriented (XState v5 statecharts; React 18; Hono; TypeScript strict)
scope_boundary: ui/ (NOT frontend/) + auth-proxy + shared/ui-state-wire + ui-state + compose ONLY. This is the FINAL closure slice carrying the single-cut closed-union deploy. NON-GOALS: backend (frozen pure-resource store since CDO-S2 — NO backend file touched in S5); frontend/ (permanent non-goal); project naming during onboarding; member invites; multi-org; changing the /state transport (ADR-046).
ratification_amendments: Amendment 2 (DISPLAY RULE — BINDING): cause tags are machine-readable wire values, NEVER rendered raw. org_name_taken/org_name_invalid → friendly inline helper text the client owns; org_create_failed/project_create_failed → a distinct 'something went wrong on our end' surface with a retry affordance. The shipped onboarding.tsx 'Cause: {cause}' rendering is THE anti-pattern this slice kills.

Amendment 3 (CONSOLE-LOG AUDIT TRAIL): the onboarding-driver logs each posted outcome event + the resulting region state via createLogger (ui/app/lib/log.ts) so the console narrates the user's flow. NEVER console.* directly.

Outcome events are PLAIN PAST TENSE (DR-2 override): org_found/org_not_found/org_created; scope_resolved/no_projects_found/project_created; session_list_loaded/session_resumed/session_created/dataset_context_switched (+ *_failed partners).
INV-PCO: the client never reads ui-state as a resource oracle; only definitive SSOT answers (200/404) are reportable (probe transport failures → bounded local re-probe, no report).

reconciliations.wire_union_additive_for_session_chat: CDO-S5 ADDS the SessionChatWireEvent OUTCOME members (session_list_loaded/session_resumed/session_created/dataset_context_switched + their *_failed partners) to the closed union. It does NOT retire org_form_submitted / create_project_submitted / switching_project_intent — those three legacy members remain because they are STILL CONSUMED by the live tests/acceptance/project-and-chat-session-management suite (switching_project_intent, create_project_submitted), by tests/acceptance/user-flow-state-machines, by frontend/app/lib/state-proxy.test.tsx, and by the ui-state router ACL as known-but-unhandled→200 convergence members (project-context machine.ts has NO switching_project state — they already converge harmlessly). ui/ STOPS PRODUCING org_form_submitted/create_project_submitted (it drives POST+report now), so the members go consumer-less from ui/ but the union keeps them for the still-live foreign consumers. Their full retirement is a future cleanup once those suites migrate — NOT this slice. The slice title 'shared (closure complete)' refers to completing the session-chat OUTCOME vocabulary, not removing the project-switch intent. (Same additive-reconciliation pattern S1 used; see cdo-s1/wave-notes.md.)
reconciliations.ui_chat_is_catalog_driven: The ui/ chat surface (Chat.tsx, ChatSessionList, metadataApiSource/catalog) drives its OWN backend fetches via the catalog abstraction and does NOT route session list/resume/create through ui-state's session-chat machine. Retiring ui-state's four session-chat egress actors is therefore a ui-state-INTERNAL cleanup (the DR-8 zero-egress mandate) that does NOT affect ui/'s working catalog-driven chat. The roadmap's 'ui/ chat surface gains probe/report choreography' deliverable is reinterpreted: the WIRE VOCABULARY (the SessionChatWireEvent outcome members) is added so the report-driven machine is representable, but NO new ui/ chat probe/report wiring is load-bearing for this slice's verification (ui/ chat already works e2e via catalog). Adding dead choreography to a working catalog-driven chat would be gold-plating. Documented in cdo-s5/wave-notes.md.
reconciliations.no_new_python_acceptance_scenario: The cdo_s5 pytest marker description states 'no new python acceptance scenario'. The workos-mode org-create interception is verified by auth-proxy UNIT tests (injected-fetch fault injection: pre-check 409, WorkOS-egress 502, membership-fail compensation, backend-non-201 compensation, compensation-fail alert). The org-onboarding python suite is the INTEGRATION REGRESSION GATE: the walking skeleton + all cdo_s1/cdo_s2/cdo_s3/cdo_s4 scenarios must stay GREEN against the rebuilt stack (post compose env deltas). The mode-discovery + reissue workos arms remain compose-gated/unit-covered per DWD-6 — honor their markers.
reconciliations.upstream_s3_1_carried_forward: test_org_name_taken_reedit.py (cdo_s3) is RED due to UPSTREAM-S3-1 (backend AuthorizationError→500 on a second org-create + a DISTILL single-principal test-design limitation — see cdo-s3/upstream-issues.md). BOTH causes are in backend + DISTILL, OUT of CDO-S5 scope (no backend file is touched in S5; the Iron Rule forbids editing the test). It shipped RED+documented at the CDO-S4 gate and is carried forward identically. Flag prominently in the completion report; do NOT modify the test.
reconciliations.api_auth_mode_override_pin_already_gone: The docker-compose.override.yml api block already has NO AUTH_MODE pin (only build/image/volumes) — the 'delete the api AUTH_MODE pin' cleanup is already satisfied in this worktree. auth-proxy.environment.AUTH_MODE stays (auth-proxy is the sole AUTH_MODE reader — correct). The remaining S5 compose work is the docker-compose.yml BASE env deltas + the auth-proxy WORKOS_BASE pin.

test_commands.ui_state_unit: cd ui-state && npx vitest run  (node_modules symlinked from main checkout — DO NOT npm install)
test_commands.auth_proxy_unit: cd auth-proxy && SKIP_DOCKER_ACCEPTANCE=1 npx vitest run  (remove dashboard-chat/{agent,auth-proxy}:bazel images first if present)
test_commands.backend: cd backend && uv run pytest  (gate runs this anyway; S5 touches no backend file → expected no-op delta)
test_commands.acceptance_suite: cd tests/acceptance/org-onboarding && REVERSE_PROXY_URL=http://localhost:1042 AUTH_PROXY_URL=http://localhost:1042 uv run --no-project --with 'httpx>=0.27,<1' --with 'pytest>=7,<9' --with 'pytest-asyncio>=0.23,<1' pytest -p no:cacheprovider --no-header -q

stack_note: NO compose stack is running; ports are free; bring up your own from THIS worktree (project defaults to 'chauffeur'). Recipe: mkdir -p backend/data && chmod 777 backend/data (the root-perms 401 trap); AUTH_MODE=dev docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.override.yml -f docker-compose.dev-no-org.yml up -d --build auth-proxy api ui-state redis query-engine. auth-proxy is the ingress on :1042. REBUILD ui-state AND auth-proxy images after code changes (up -d --build <svc>). Between acceptance reruns: the fresh_dev_principal fixture self-cleans created_by=dev-user-001 orgs/projects; if state is sticky: redis-cli -n 1 flushdb + docker restart of ui-state. Done → docker compose ... down (leave images).
des_gate_gaps: The refinery --auto gate runs backend pytest ONLY. Your local greens are the ONLY pre-merge verification for ALL TypeScript (ui-state, auth-proxy, ui). MANDATORY before submit: ui-state vitest, auth-proxy vitest, ui vitest, ui typecheck, backend pytest, + the org-onboarding acceptance suite against the rebuilt stack.

design_context_shared.single_cut: shared/ui-state-wire + ui-state + ui/ + auth-proxy + compose must be internally consistent in ONE merge (ADR-050 §e cost note). The env deltas + any override cleanup land HERE so the stack stays coherent. Sequence: ui-state code (05-01/02) before the compose ui-state env removal (05-06) so the container still boots; config.ts must be Redis-only BEFORE BACKEND_URL/FAKE_WORKOS_URL are removed from compose.
design_context_shared.transport_unchanged: ADR-046 /state surface (GET /ui-state/state, POST /ui-state/state/events) + StateProxy + StateProxyProvider are BYTE-untouched. The closed union only tightens the ChatAppWireEvent parameter at compile time.
design_context_shared.status_to_cause_mapping: ui/ client maps POST /api/orgs result → outcome report: 201 {data:{id,attributes:{name}}}→org_created{org:{id,name}}; 409→org_create_failed{cause:org_name_taken,org_name}; 400|422→org_create_failed{cause:org_name_invalid,org_name}; any other/network/timeout→org_create_failed{cause:org_create_failed}; 401→NOT a report (auth gate /login). POST /api/projects: 201→project_created{project:{id,name}}; 401→auth gate; else→project_create_failed{cause:project_create_failed}.
design_context_shared.retry_policies: org_name_taken/org_name_invalid → no retry, user re-edits, submit re-POSTs (renders org_validation_error inline). org_create_failed → MANUAL retry only (org create not idempotent end-to-end, ADR-048 R5); error_recoverable offers a retry affordance; retry = re-POST same name → re-report. project_create_failed → MANUAL retry with PROBE-FIRST CONVERGENCE: re-probe GET /api/projects before re-POST; non-empty → report scope_resolved (lost-201 dedup); empty → re-POST + re-report. Probe transport failure (Phase B GET /api/orgs/me 5xx/network) → NOT reportable; bounded local re-probe; document stays awaiting_org_report.
design_context_shared.engaged_flip_f_triple: The client enters the app on the response document of its OWN project_created (or scope_resolved) POST when regions.projectContext.state === 'project_selected' AND active_scope.project_id is non-null; phase === 'chat' is routing convenience only. The parent's isInitialProjectSelected guard advances engaged.project_context → chat in the SAME settle. Duplicate/stale/out-of-order/crash-recovery all converge (no handler → no transition → current document returned → navigation idempotent). StateProxy/StateProxyProvider UNTOUCHED.
design_context_shared.interception_workflow: auth-proxy POST /api/orgs in workos mode: (i) pre-check GET /api/orgs/availability with identity headers + correlation id BEFORE any WorkOS egress — a 409-class answer synthesizes the backend JSON:API 409 shape, never touches WorkOS; (ii) WorkOS org-create + membership via lib/user-auth/workos.ts (WORKOS_BASE, injected fetch, 5s AbortSignal per call, NO auto-retry create, 1 retry membership); (iii) forward to backend with X-Provisioned-Org-Id (joins IDENTITY_HEADERS strip list — strip-then-inject); (iv) backend non-201 after WorkOS success → best-effort compensation DELETE /organizations/{id} (1 retry); compensation fail → alertable workos.org_compensate.fail with the orphan id; (v) WorkOS-egress failures synthesize 502 {errors:[{status:'502',title:'Organization provisioning failed',code:'org_provisioning_failed'}]}. Dev mode forwards straight through (zero-overhead path+method guard). Emit org_create.intercepted. Read the inbound body name via c.req.raw.clone().json() so the forward leg still streams. Insert the branch between the identity-header inject (app.ts ~802) and proxyRequest (~804). Compensation stays best-effort (UPSTREAM-3 RESOLVED: WorkOS does NOT enforce org-name uniqueness; A+B layered).
design_context_shared.observability_emit: auth-proxy emits stdout-JSON-line events via the existing emitKpiEvent({event,...}) helper. CDO-S5 events: org_create.intercepted, auth.reissue.emitted (transport:'both' — already emitted by the reissue hook from S4; confirm), workos.org_compensate.fail (carries the orphan id).
design_context_shared.ui_state_zero_egress: After S5, ui-state has ZERO network egress (grep-clean of backendUrl/workosUrl/fetch egress). The session-chat machine becomes report-driven; the four session-chat egress actor functions are DELETED; the already-dead onboarding (loadSession/createOrg) and project-context (resolveInitialScope/createProject/switchProject) egress actor functions are DELETED; config.ts shrinks to Redis-only (drop workosUrl/backendUrl/devUserHeadersFixture); index.ts drops the buildChatAppDeps egress wiring; OnboardingInput.config (unused since the onboarding machine went report-driven in S1) is dropped.
design_context_shared.createLogger_api: ui/app/lib/log.ts exports createLogger(channel): Logger with debug/info/warn/error(action: string, attributes?: Record<string,unknown>). action is a dotted stable key (e.g. 'onboarding.org_created.reported'). NEVER console.*.
design_context_shared.backendClient_ApiError: ui/app/catalog/dataSources/backendClient.ts apiGet/apiPost today throw plain Error(message) on non-2xx. EXTEND so the thrown error is an ApiError carrying {status:number, body:unknown} for the driver's status→cause dispatch. The catalog's own call sites must keep working (an Error subclass with the same message keeps them green).

phases.name: CDO-S5 closure: ui/ client-drive surfaces + auth-proxy workos org-create interception + ui-state zero-egress

steps.name: Session-chat DR-8: report-driven realignment + egress retirement + wire outcome members
steps.scenario_name: report-driven session-chat transitions (loadSessionList/resume/create/switch become client reports)
steps.criteria: ADD the SessionChatWireEvent OUTCOME members to the shared closed union (session_list_loaded{sessions,next_cursor,has_more}, session_resumed{session_id,transcript,resource?,session_dataset_unavailable?}, session_created{session}, dataset_context_switched{resource} + session_list_failed/session_resume_failed/session_create_failed/dataset_context_switch_failed each {cause}). Realign the session-chat machine to transition on those reports instead of invoke onDone: region renames loading_session_list→awaiting_session_list_report; REMOVE resuming_session/creating_session/switching_dataset_context states; the surviving UI intents (session_clicked/new_session_clicked/first_message_sent/refresh_session_list/dataset_resolved_by_agent/dataset_picked_directly/suggestion chips) still trigger the (now report-accepting) transitions; error_recoverable accepts the *_failed reports. DELETE the four egress actor functions (loadSessionListFn/resumeSessionFn/createSessionEagerlyFn/switchDatasetContextFn) from session-chat/setup/actors.ts and remove their invoke wiring. The router ACL gains Zod arms for the new outcome members (closed z.ZodType<ChatAppWireEvent>). KEEP org_form_submitted/create_project_submitted/switching_project_intent in the union + ACL (still-live foreign consumers — see reconciliations). retry_clicked already retired (S3/S4).
files_to_modify: shared/ui-state-wire/wire-event.ts — ADD a SessionChatWireEvent union (outcome members: session_list_loaded/session_resumed/session_created/dataset_context_switched + their *_failed{cause} partners; cause type SessionChatFailureCause as a string-literal union mirroring the field names). The surviving session-chat UI intents already exist; keep them. Reference the member as `| SessionChatWireEvent` in ChatAppWireEvent. ADDITIVE only — do NOT remove the three legacy members.

shared/ui-state-wire/state-document.ts — sessionChat region state set: rename loading_session_list→awaiting_session_list_report; remove resuming_session/creating_session/switching_dataset_context from the documented set; keep waiting_for_project/session_list_loaded/session_welcome/session_active/error_recoverable. anonymousStateDocument sessionChat zero state stays waiting_for_project.

ui-state/lib/machines/session-chat/machine.ts — realign report-driven per §e.5: awaiting_session_list_report (no invoke) ← project_ready; on session_list_loaded→session_list_loaded(state)/on session_list_failed→error_recoverable; session_clicked→(report-accepting) on session_resumed→session_active / session_resume_failed→error_recoverable; first_message_sent→on session_created→session_active / session_create_failed→error_recoverable; dataset_resolved_by_agent/dataset_picked_directly→on dataset_context_switched→session_active / dataset_context_switch_failed→error_recoverable. REMOVE the resuming_session/creating_session/switching_dataset_context invoke states. Preserve waiting_for_project/session_welcome/session_active/error_recoverable + the stale-click/welcome-reset guards.

ui-state/lib/machines/session-chat/setup/types.ts — SessionChatState drops resuming_session/creating_session/switching_dataset_context, renames loading_session_list→awaiting_session_list_report; SessionChatEvent gains the outcome members (payloads = the retired invoke OUTPUT types verbatim, INV-PCO display data); keep the surviving intents; retry_clicked may stay if still referenced by error_recoverable, else drop.

ui-state/lib/machines/session-chat/setup/actors.ts — DELETE loadSessionListFn/resumeSessionFn/createSessionEagerlyFn/switchDatasetContextFn (+ their *Actor factories) and any fetch/backendUrl reference; keep only pure helper/type exports the machine still needs (assign actions move to setup/actions.ts if present). The OUTPUT type shapes become the report payload types — preserve them as exported types if the wire/types reference them.

ui-state/lib/machines/session-chat/setup/actions.ts — assignSessionList/assignResumedSession/assignCreatedSession/assignSwitchedDataset read event payload instead of invoke output; tagListDegraded/tagTransient* read the *_failed cause; remove actions that only served the deleted invokes.

ui-state/lib/machines/session-chat/setup/guards.ts — drop guards that only gated invoke onDone branches (isSessionNotFound/isDatasetAccessDenied move to the report payload if still needed, or retire); keep stale-click/different-project guards.

ui-state/lib/machines/chat-app/router.ts — extend the closed chatAppWireEventSchema with Zod arms for the new session-chat outcome members (well-formedness only); the _wireEventSchemaPin stays z.ZodType<ChatAppWireEvent>.

ui-state/lib/machines/chat-app/projection/derive-state-document.ts — SESSION_CHAT_STATE_MAP: add awaiting_session_list_report, drop dead resuming_session/creating_session/switching_dataset_context; deriveSessionChat fallback unchanged (waiting_for_project).
ui-state/lib/machines/chat-app/snapshot.ts — drop the deleted session-chat invoke states from the transient/not-settled set so awaiting_session_list_report settles.

ui-state/lib/machines/session-chat/machine.test.ts — REPLACE the invoke-onDone/onError tests with report-driven transition tests: awaiting_session_list_report on session_list_loaded→session_list_loaded; on session_list_failed→error_recoverable; session_clicked→on session_resumed→session_active; first_message_sent→on session_created→session_active; dataset intent→on dataset_context_switched→session_active; each *_failed→error_recoverable. Keep the stale-click/welcome/project-switch tests green.

ui-state/lib/machines/session-chat/setup/actors.test.ts (if present) — the mock-fetch request_client actor tests for the four egress actors DIE with the actors; remove them (sanctioned rework — the egress they tested is retired).
steps.implementation_notes: Outside-In: the OUTER loop here is ui-state vitest (the python acceptance suite has no session-chat scenario — it is the 05-06 integration regression). RED_UNIT: drive session-chat/machine.test.ts report-driven cases (currently the machine invokes; the new tests assert transitions on reports). GREEN: minimal machine + wire + router + projection + snapshot. Run `cd ui-state && npx vitest run` — FULL ui-state suite must be green (chat-app integration + onboarding + project-context untouched). The session-chat actor egress deletion is the zero-egress down-payment; the onboarding/project-context dead-actor deletion + config shrink is 05-02. Keep the three legacy wire members. Append cdo-s5/wave-notes.md documenting the additive-wire reconciliation + the ui-chat-is-catalog-driven note.

steps.name: ui-state zero-egress cleanup: delete dead egress actors + Redis-only config
steps.scenario_name: ui-state boots + cold-starts + settles with NO network egress (Redis-only config)
steps.criteria: ui-state has ZERO network egress: grep over ui-state/ (excluding node_modules + tests) for backendUrl/workosUrl/`fetch(` returns nothing live. DELETE the now-dead egress actor functions in onboarding/setup/actors.ts (loadSession/loadVerifiedSession + createOrg/createOrgFn/getOrg — the machine went report-driven in S1) and project-context/setup/actors.ts (resolveInitialScopeFn/createProjectFn/switchProjectFn — already not invoked since S1/S3). config.ts shrinks to Redis-only (drop workosUrl/backendUrl/devUserHeadersFixture). index.ts drops the buildChatAppDeps egress wiring (sessionChat block already gone in 05-01; remove the projectContext block + the backendUrl/headers locals + the unused actor imports) and stops threading config/requestClient where they only fed retired egress. OnboardingInput.config (unused) is dropped. The full ui-state vitest suite stays green; the chat-app cold-start + walking-skeleton-equivalent integration test still settles.
files_to_modify: ui-state/lib/machines/onboarding/setup/actors.ts — DELETE the egress actor functions (loadSession/loadVerifiedSession with the WorkOS userinfo + /api/orgs/me fetch; createOrg/getOrg/createOrgFn with the /api/orgs POST). Remove all workosUrl/backendUrl/request_client references. If the file becomes empty of live exports, keep only the types the machine still imports (or delete the file and fix imports).
ui-state/lib/machines/onboarding/setup/types.ts — drop OnboardingInput.config (workosUrl/backendUrl) + any deps.request_client field now unused; keep user:{email,display_name,first_name}.

ui-state/lib/machines/onboarding/machine.ts — remove any residual actors map entries / invoke references to the deleted actors (should already be report-driven from S1; this is dead-reference cleanup only).

ui-state/lib/machines/project-context/setup/actors.ts — DELETE resolveInitialScopeFn/createProjectFn/switchProjectFn + their *Actor factories + all backendUrl/fetch references (buildActors already returns {}). Keep only types still imported elsewhere.
ui-state/lib/machines/project-context/setup/types.ts — drop the retired actor deps fields (resolveInitialScope/createProject/switchProject) from ProjectContextMachineDeps if they remain.
ui-state/lib/machines/project-context/index.ts — remove the resolveInitialScopeActor/createProjectActor/switchProjectActor exports if they only re-export deleted fns.

ui-state/config.ts — Redis-only: DELETE workosUrl (FAKE_WORKOS_URL), backendUrl (BACKEND_URL), devUserHeadersFixture; keep redisUrl + any genuinely-Redis config. Update the Zod env schema accordingly.

ui-state/index.ts — remove buildChatAppDeps egress wiring (the projectContext block + backendUrl/headers locals + the retired actor imports); stop forwarding config (workosUrl/backendUrl) + requestClient into OnboardingInput/runtime where they only fed retired egress. The actor cold-start no longer needs config.
ui-state/lib/testing/test-config.ts — drop the workosUrl/backendUrl test helpers (or stub to Redis-only) so the test infra compiles.

ui-state machine/integration tests + fixtures — remove the mock-fetch request_client fixtures / test doubles anchored by the deleted actors (sanctioned rework); the chat-app integration + snapshot tests that referenced resolveInitialScope/createProject as injected deps drop those injections.
steps.implementation_notes: Outside-In: OUTER loop ui-state vitest. This is primarily DELETE + SHRINK (dead-code removal — the onboarding machine is report-driven since S1, project-context invokes retired since S1/S3, session-chat egress deleted in 05-01). The risk is the test-double fallout: 'every mock-fetch request_client fixture the deleted actors anchor' dies — rework those fixtures, do NOT re-introduce egress to keep them green (Iron Rule on the tests means: if a test asserts the egress contract, it retires WITH the actor — that is sanctioned, the actor is deleted by design, not to make a test pass). After GREEN: `cd ui-state && npx vitest run` FULL green, AND run `grep -rnE 'backendUrl|workosUrl|fetch\(' ui-state/lib ui-state/config.ts ui-state/index.ts | grep -v node_modules | grep -v '.test.'` → MUST be empty (the zero-egress proof). Append the grep-clean evidence to cdo-s5/wave-notes.md. Compose env removal (BACKEND_URL/FAKE_WORKOS_URL/extra_hosts) is 05-06 and depends on this step's config shrink.

steps.name: auth-proxy WorkOS org-create interception (pre-check → provision → forward → compensate)
steps.scenario_name: org-create interception: pre-check 409, provision, carry X-Provisioned-Org-Id, compensate, timeouts (injected fetch)
steps.criteria: In AUTH_MODE=workos, POST /api/orgs is intercepted: (i) pre-check GET /api/orgs/availability?name= with the same identity headers + correlation id BEFORE any WorkOS egress — taken → synthesize the backend JSON:API 409 shape, NO WorkOS egress; (ii) WorkOS org-create then membership via workos.ts (WORKOS_BASE, injected fetch, AbortSignal.timeout(5000) per call, NO auto-retry on create, 1 retry on membership); (iii) forward to backend with X-Provisioned-Org-Id:<workos_org_id> (the header joins IDENTITY_HEADERS so a client-supplied value is stripped on every route — strip-then-inject); (iv) backend non-201 after WorkOS success → best-effort compensation DELETE /organizations/{id} (1 retry); compensation failure → emit workos.org_compensate.fail{orphan_id} and still relay the backend status; (v) any WorkOS-egress failure (timeout/4xx/5xx) → synthesize 502 {errors:[{status:'502',title:'Organization provisioning failed',code:'org_provisioning_failed'}]}. Dev mode (AUTH_MODE!=workos) forwards straight through with zero overhead (path+method guard). Emit org_create.intercepted. The forward leg still streams (read name via c.req.raw.clone().json()).
files_to_modify: auth-proxy/lib/user-auth/workos.ts — ADD org-provisioning ops on the existing injected-fetch boundary (this.fetchPort, this.config.baseUrl=WORKOS_BASE, this.config.clientSecret=WORKOS_API_KEY): createOrganization(name)→{id}; createOrganizationMembership(userId,orgId); deleteOrganization(orgId). Each wraps a private call helper with AbortSignal.timeout(5000); map 401/non-ok/throw to a typed failure ('service_error'); NO auto-retry on create; the caller does the 1-retry on membership + delete.

auth-proxy/lib/org-create-workflow.ts — NEW. runOrgCreateInterception({ req, identityHeaders, correlationId, name, userId, deps:{ workos, preCheckAvailability, forwardToBackend, emit } }): orchestrate pre-check→provision→forward→compensate per the criteria; return a Response (synthesized 409/502 or the relayed backend response). Pure of Hono internals where feasible (inject the collaborators) so it is fault-injection unit-testable. Emit org_create.intercepted + workos.org_compensate.fail via the injected emit.
auth-proxy/lib/auth.ts — add 'x-provisioned-org-id' to IDENTITY_HEADERS (strip-then-inject; a client value is dropped on every route).

auth-proxy/app.ts — insert the interception branch in app.all('*') between the identity-header inject (~802) and proxyRequest (~804): if c.req.method==='POST' && pathname==='/api/orgs' && AUTH_MODE==='workos' → read name via c.req.raw.clone().json(), build the availability pre-check + backend-forward closures (reusing proxyRequest/the existing fetch-to-backend wiring + the injected identity headers incl. X-Provisioned-Org-Id), call runOrgCreateInterception, return its Response (the post-response applyOrgCreateReissue still runs on a relayed 201). Dev mode falls through unchanged. Wire a WorkOsUserAuthProvider (or its org-provisioning subset) for the workflow using createProviderForRequest's config.

auth-proxy/lib/user-auth/workos.test.ts — ADD unit tests for createOrganization/createOrganizationMembership/deleteOrganization via the constructor-injected mockFetch: success, 5s-timeout (AbortSignal), WorkOS 4xx/5xx → service_error, membership 1-retry.

auth-proxy/lib/org-create-workflow.test.ts — NEW. Fault-injected collaborators: pre-check taken→synthesized backend-409 (no workos calls made); workos create fail→502; membership fail after retry→502 + compensation delete attempted; backend non-201→compensation delete (1 retry) + status relayed; compensation delete fail→workos.org_compensate.fail emitted with orphan id + status still relayed; happy path→X-Provisioned-Org-Id carried on the forward + 201 relayed + org_create.intercepted emitted. Assert NO WorkOS egress on the pre-check-409 path.

auth-proxy/app.test.ts — ADD an interception integration arm (AUTH_MODE=workos, stubbed global fetch for backend + WorkOS): POST /api/orgs pre-check + provision + forward carries x-provisioned-org-id; AND a strip test: a client-supplied X-Provisioned-Org-Id on ANY route is stripped. Keep the dev-mode straight-forward path asserted (no interception when AUTH_MODE=dev).
steps.implementation_notes: Outside-In: OUTER loop is auth-proxy vitest (the python suite has no cdo_s5 workos scenario — DWD-6). Drive org-create-workflow.test.ts (the pre-check/provision/forward/compensate matrix) first; then the workos.ts op unit tests; then the app.ts integration arm. The fake-WorkOS compose seam (WORKOS_BASE pin) is wired in 05-06 but is NOT required for these unit tests (injected fetch covers it). Run `cd auth-proxy && SKIP_DOCKER_ACCEPTANCE=1 npx vitest run` — green (remove dashboard-chat/{agent,auth-proxy}:bazel images first if loaded). The post-response applyOrgCreateReissue (S4) is REUSED unchanged — confirm it still fires on the relayed 201 and confirm auth.reissue.emitted transport:'both'. emitKpiEvent is the existing stdout-JSON-line helper. KEEP the cheap path+method guard so every non-org-create proxied request is zero-overhead. Append the interception design + the compensation-best-effort note (UPSTREAM-3 resolved) to cdo-s5/wave-notes.md.

steps.name: ui/ driver foundation: onboarding-driver + ApiError + fetchAuthConfig
steps.scenario_name: onboarding-driver pure policy: status→cause mapping, definitive-answers-only probe, probe-first convergence, createLogger narration
steps.criteria: NEW ui/app/lib/onboarding-driver.ts owns the relocated flow policy as a PURE, browser-free module (collaborators injected: a backend client, a report sink, a logger): Phase-B probe GET /api/orgs/me with the DEFINITIVE-ANSWERS-ONLY rule (only 200→org_found{org} and 404→org_not_found are reportable; transport errors → bounded local re-probe, NO report); status→cause mapping for org-create (201→org_created; 409→org_name_taken; 400/422→org_name_invalid; else→org_create_failed; 401→auth gate, never a report); AUTOMATIC Phase-D default project POST /api/projects {name:'My First Project'} (no user input); retry policies (manual-only org create; project = probe-first convergence: re-probe GET /api/projects, non-empty→scope_resolved, empty→re-POST→project_created/project_create_failed); initial-scope resolution ported from the retired resolveInitialScopeFn (200 project→scope_resolved, empty→no_projects_found). It logs each POSTED outcome event + the resulting region state via createLogger (amendment 3 — NEVER console.*). backendClient throws an ApiError {status, body} so the mapping can dispatch (the catalog's existing call sites stay green). bootstrap gains fetchAuthConfig() (memoized; Zod-validated {mode:'dev'|'workos'} at the boundary).
files_to_modify: ui/app/catalog/dataSources/backendClient.ts — define `export class ApiError extends Error { constructor(public status:number, public body:unknown, message?:string){...} }`; apiGet/apiPost/apiPatch/apiUpload throw `new ApiError(status, parsedBodyOrNull, \`...\`)` on non-2xx instead of a plain Error (keep the message text so existing catalog call sites that read .message stay green).

ui/app/auth/bootstrap.ts — ADD fetchAuthConfig(): Promise<{mode:'dev'|'workos'}> — GET /api/auth/config, Zod-parse the body at the boundary (z.object({mode:z.enum(['dev','workos'])}).passthrough()), memoize the resolved promise module-level (fetched at most once per app load). login()/handleCallback()/extractCode() unchanged.

ui/app/lib/onboarding-driver.ts — NEW. Export a factory (e.g. createOnboardingDriver({ client, report, log })) or pure functions: probeAndReportOrg(), reportOrgCreateResult(httpResultOrError), createDefaultProjectAndReport(), resolveInitialScopeAndReport(), retryOrgCreate(), retryProject() (probe-first). Map HTTP/ApiError → the past-tense ChatAppWireEvent (status→cause table). Apply the definitive-answers-only rule on the probe. Log each posted event {type, region_state_after?} via the injected createLogger('onboarding-driver'). Import the wire types from @dashboard-chat/ui-state-wire. NO direct DOM/React — testable with injected collaborators.

ui/app/lib/onboarding-driver.test.ts — NEW. Pure unit tests (no browser): 201→org_created{org}; 409→org_create_failed{cause:org_name_taken,org_name}; 422→org_name_invalid; 400→org_name_invalid; 500/network→org_create_failed{cause:org_create_failed}; 401→NO report (auth-gate signal); probe 200→org_found, 404→org_not_found, 5xx/network→NO report (bounded re-probe); project probe-first: non-empty list→scope_resolved (no duplicate POST), empty→POST 201→project_created; initial-scope 200→scope_resolved, empty→no_projects_found; assert createLogger received an entry per posted event (inject a spy logger).
ui/app/auth/bootstrap.test.ts (or extend existing) — fetchAuthConfig memoizes (one fetch for repeated calls), Zod-rejects a malformed body, returns {mode} on success.
steps.implementation_notes: Outside-In: OUTER loop ui vitest. Drive onboarding-driver.test.ts first (the status→cause matrix + definitive-answers-only + probe-first convergence are the policy SSOT). The driver is PURE policy (DR-1: in-flight UI is the surface's local concern, not the driver's) — inject the backend client (so ApiError mapping is testable), the report sink (proxy.postEvent), and the logger. Reuse @dashboard-chat/ui-state-wire types for the event shapes (closed union from 05-01). Run `cd ui && npx vitest run` green + `cd ui && npm run typecheck` clean for the new/changed files. The surfaces (login/onboarding/app-shell) consume this driver in 05-05. Append the driver policy notes to cdo-s5/wave-notes.md.

steps.name: ui/ surfaces: login mode-discovery + onboarding POST+report + app-shell gate
steps.scenario_name: org form drives POST+report; ProjectNameForm gone; friendly copy (no raw cause tag); retry affordance; login renders per mode; app-shell gate
steps.criteria: login.tsx: render NO sign-in affordance until fetchAuthConfig resolves; dev button ONLY when mode==='dev'; plain 'Sign in' when mode==='workos'; both invoke the unchanged login(). onboarding.tsx: the org form drives the real POST /api/orgs via the driver then reports (in-flight UI is LOCAL useState — the document never shows an in-flight org state); ProjectNameForm is DELETED (Phase D is automatic via the driver's createDefaultProjectAndReport, triggered on entering the project phase); the error surface obeys the BINDING DISPLAY RULE — org_name_taken/org_name_invalid render user-friendly inline helper text the client owns (NOT the raw tag), and the retry class (org_create_failed/project_create_failed) renders a distinct 'something went wrong on our end' surface with a retry affordance; NEVER render a raw cause tag (kill the 'Cause: {cause}' rendering). The state arms update: verifying→awaiting_org_report; creating_org/session_rejected arms die. app-shell.tsx: ONBOARDING_ACTIVE_STATES → {needs_org, error_recoverable}; the phase==='rejected' branch dies; waits on awaiting_org_report; after bootstrap, invoke the driver's Phase-B probe when the document shows awaiting_org_report; no_projects routing survives (driver auto-creates from /onboarding). The project_selected→refreshOrgGlobal()→navigate('/') effect survives verbatim (the (f) contract).
files_to_modify: ui/app/routes/login.tsx — on mount, fetch the memoized auth config (fetchAuthConfig from bootstrap); until resolved render a neutral waiting surface (no sign-in affordance — no flash of a dev button in workos mode); mode==='dev'→the 'Sign in (dev)' button; mode==='workos'→a plain 'Sign in' button; both onClick call the unchanged login(). Keep the hasSession()→Navigate('/') short-circuit.

ui/app/routes/onboarding.tsx — OrgNameForm submit → driver.reportOrgCreateResult(await client POST /api/orgs) with an in-flight LOCAL useState (busy) — NOT a document state; on a re-edit cause the inline org_validation_error renders friendly helper text the client owns. DELETE ProjectNameForm (and its create_project_submitted post); the project phase surface becomes a progress view driven by the driver's automatic createDefaultProjectAndReport (trigger it once on entering project_context/awaiting_scope_report). Switch arms: verifying→awaiting_org_report; needs_org→OrgNameForm; error_recoverable→the generic 'something went wrong on our end' retry surface (retry affordance → driver.retryOrgCreate / retryProject); REMOVE the creating_org/session_rejected arms. DELETE the ErrorSurface 'Cause: {cause}' rendering — replace with the display-rule copy. Keep the project_selected→refreshOrgGlobal()→navigate('/') effect verbatim. Use createLogger for any narration.

ui/app/routes/app-shell.tsx — ONBOARDING_ACTIVE_STATES = {needs_org, error_recoverable}; DELETE the phase==='rejected' Navigate branch; the wait state becomes awaiting_org_report (also the anonymous-document zero state); after ensureBootstrap, when the document shows onboarding awaiting_org_report, invoke the driver's Phase-B probe (probeAndReportOrg); keep the no_projects→/onboarding routing (the driver auto-creates from there).

ui/app/routes/onboarding.test.tsx — REWORK: assert OrgNameForm drives a POST (scriptedStateProxy/injected client) and posts org_created on 201 / org_create_failed{cause} on 409·422; assert the re-edit cause renders FRIENDLY inline copy (NOT the raw tag string) and the retry class renders the generic surface + a retry control; assert ProjectNameForm is GONE and the project phase auto-creates; assert NO raw cause tag text is rendered anywhere a failure shows.

ui/app/routes/login.test.tsx — REWORK: no sign-in affordance before fetchAuthConfig resolves; dev button rendered only when mode==='dev'; plain 'Sign in' when mode==='workos'; both call login(); inject a stubbed fetchAuthConfig/config fetch.

ui/app/routes/app-shell.test.tsx (if present) — update the gate expectations: awaiting_org_report wait; no rejected branch; {needs_org, error_recoverable} active set; the probe fires on awaiting_org_report.
steps.implementation_notes: Outside-In: OUTER loop ui vitest. RED first on onboarding.test.tsx (the DISPLAY RULE assertion — friendly copy, never a raw cause tag — is the headline) then login.test.tsx then app-shell. Tests use createMemoryRouter + RouterProvider + StateProxyProvider with scriptedStateProxy (the posted[] array is the event-contract assertion target) per the existing test kit. The driver (05-04) is the policy; these surfaces are thin React consumers — keep the in-flight UI LOCAL (DR-1), never a document state. Run `cd ui && npx vitest run` green + `cd ui && npm run typecheck` clean (the closed union from 05-01 tightens the postEvent parameter — fix any type fallout HERE). Confirm the (f)-triple navigation effect is byte-preserved. Append the display-rule + audit-trail evidence to cdo-s5/wave-notes.md.

steps.name: Compose env deltas (single-cut) + full acceptance integration gate
steps.scenario_name: the rebuilt single-cut stack keeps the walking skeleton + cdo_s1..s4 GREEN (integration regression)
steps.criteria: docker-compose.yml env deltas land per ADR-048 §4: the api + api-full service blocks lose AUTH_MODE + WORKOS_* (harmless — backend config is already grep-clean since CDO-S2; this is cleanup); the ui-state service block loses FAKE_WORKOS_URL + AUTH_MODE + BACKEND_URL + extra_hosts (ui-state is Redis-only + zero-egress after 05-02 — the container must still boot); the auth-proxy service block GAINS WORKOS_BASE: ${WORKOS_BASE:-https://api.workos.com} (the relocated fake-WorkOS R4 seam). docker-compose.override.yml: the api AUTH_MODE pin is already absent (no-op) — leave auth-proxy.environment.AUTH_MODE (the sole reader). After the deltas, the rebuilt stack (ui-state + auth-proxy images rebuilt) boots and the FULL org-onboarding acceptance suite is GREEN for the walking skeleton + every cdo_s1/cdo_s2/cdo_s3/cdo_s4 scenario, EXCEPT the carried-forward UPSTREAM-S3-1 RED (test_org_name_taken_reedit — backend AuthorizationError→500 + DISTILL single-principal limitation, both out of S5 scope, Iron-Rule-protected). The cdo_s5 marker has no python scenario (workos interception is auth-proxy-unit-covered).
files_to_modify: docker-compose.yml — api service: remove AUTH_MODE + WORKOS_* env keys. api-full service (if present): same. ui-state service: remove FAKE_WORKOS_URL, AUTH_MODE, BACKEND_URL env keys + the extra_hosts block. auth-proxy service: ADD WORKOS_BASE: ${WORKOS_BASE:-https://api.workos.com}. Touch ONLY these env/host keys — no topology/port/replica change (ADR-048 §5).
docker-compose.override.yml — confirm the api block has NO AUTH_MODE (already true); leave auth-proxy AUTH_MODE: dev (sole reader). No change expected unless a stray WORKOS_* lingers on api.
steps.implementation_notes: This is a config-only step (compose env deltas) — the 'test' is the integration regression: bring up the stack from THIS worktree (stack_note recipe) with ui-state + auth-proxy rebuilt, then run the FULL org-onboarding acceptance suite. RED_ACCEPTANCE here = confirm the suite still collects + the walking skeleton is the live target; GREEN = the suite passes (minus the documented UPSTREAM-S3-1 RED). DES phases: PREPARE (read compose), GREEN (apply deltas + rebuild + run suite), COMMIT. Verify the ui-state container BOOTS with the env removed (config.ts is Redis-only from 05-02 — if it still requires backendUrl/workosUrl the container crashes → that is a 05-02 regression, fix there, not by re-adding env). The auth-proxy WORKOS_BASE default is the api.workos.com production value; a fake-WorkOS workos-mode acceptance arm is OPTIONAL (auth-proxy units already cover interception) — leave any compose-gated workos arm skipped as designed. Append the suite result table (pass/skip/the one carried RED) to cdo-s5/deliver-summary.md. python3 tools/check_workspace_consistency.py is NOT needed (no package.json change) — but run it if any package.json changed across the slice.

distill_scenario_coverage.note: CDO-S5 adds NO new python acceptance scenario (cdo_s5 marker: 'no new python acceptance scenario'). The workos org-create interception is covered by auth-proxy UNIT tests (05-03: org-create-workflow.test.ts + workos.test.ts + app.test.ts arm). The ui/ onboarding-driver policy is covered by ui UNIT tests (05-04: onboarding-driver.test.ts). The ui/ surfaces are covered by ui component tests (05-05). The org-onboarding python suite is the INTEGRATION REGRESSION GATE (05-06): the walking skeleton + cdo_s1..s4 stay GREEN against the rebuilt single-cut stack.
distill_scenario_coverage.server_contracts_already_covered: CDO-S1..S4 acceptance scenarios (the suite's driver IS the client) prove the dev server contracts the ui/ driver mirrors.

out_of_s5_red_expected: test_org_name_taken_reedit.py (cdo_s3) stays RED — UPSTREAM-S3-1 (backend AuthorizationError→500 + DISTILL single-principal test-design limitation). Both causes are in backend + DISTILL, OUT of CDO-S5 scope; the Iron Rule forbids editing the test. Carried forward identically to the CDO-S4 gate. The mode-discovery + reissue WORKOS arms stay compose-gated / auth-proxy-unit-covered (DWD-6) — honor their markers.

validation.approved_by: orchestrator (nw-deliver) — automated quality gate + roadmap format pre-check (verify_deliver_integrity) + design reconciliation against ADR-048/050 + application-architecture.md; no DISTILL python scenarios for S5 (workos interception is auth-proxy-unit-covered, ui/ driver is ui-unit-covered) so orphan-scenario review is N/A; the org-onboarding suite is the integration regression gate
